title: trasus glaucus var. michelianus
intro: Trasus glaucus var. michelianus, also known as Micheli's Trasus or Micheli's Salsify, is a plant species in the genus Tragopogon. This perennial plant is native to Europe and Asia and is known for its edible roots and medicinal properties. The root of Trasus glaucus var. michelianus is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
